TWO MOBILE HOMES WITH DAMAGE... ONE SLID OFF FOUNDATION AND SECOND WITH PART OF ROOF TORN OFF NEAR INTERSECTION OF MILE 4 W AND MILE 11 N ROAD. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS KNOCKED DOWN POWER LINES NEARBY ALONG MILE 10 N ROAD.
GUST TO 42KT AT KPIL ASOS.
PORT ISABEL AND SOUTH PADRE ISLAND TCOON NOS STATIONS GUSTED TO 34KT
BROWNSVILLE POLICE REPORT SEVERAL FLOODED ROADWAYS. WATER REACHING CAR DOORS IN SOME AREAS WEST OF HIGHWAY 69.
ROAD CLOSED AT SUGAR ROAD AND POLK AVENUE IN PHARR DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
EM REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AT 8 STREET AND MISSOURI AVE IN MERCEDES.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED ROAD CLOSED ON SOUTH ALAMO ROAD SOUTH OF BUSINESS 83.
CARS LOCATED AT FRONTAGE AND STEWART ROAD IN SAN JUAN ARE HALF WAY SUBMERGED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
RIO GRANDE CITY POLICE REPORTS MAJOR STREET FLOODING WIDESPREAD ACROSS THE CITY.
RIO GRANDE CITY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS EMBASSY STREET CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. ALSO HOUSE FLOODED ON CENICA STREET. LOS OLMOS CREEK ALSO OUT OF BANKS...DEPTH OF FLOODWATER UNKNOWN.
STARR COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS LOS OLMOS CREEK IS 2 TO 3 FEET ABOVE ITS BANK IN THE LAS LOMAS AREA. LAS LOMAS NEIGHBORHOOD FLOODED...DEPTH OF FLOODWATER UNKNOWN.
RIO GRANDE CITY FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTS 9 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ON EMBASSY STREET. RGCFD ALSO REPORTS SANDBAGS DELIVERED TO 3 HOMES AND 3 VEHICLES REMOVED FROM FLOODWATERS.
EXTENSIVE STREET FLOODING IN MCALLEN. SOCIAL MEDIA POST FROM KRGV. WATER NEAR TOP OF TIRE WELLS SEEN ON SOME VEHICLES.
BROADCAST STORY REPORTED ON FLASH FLOODING THAT OCCURRED IN RURAL HIDALGO COUNTY. SEVERAL FEET OF STANDING WATER AND FLOOD WATERS DAMAGED PROPERTY.
NOAA BUOY 42020 GUSTED TO 47 KNOTS AT 830 PM.

EVIDENCE OF FLASH FLOODING ON FM 1847 AND FM 106 NEAR LA TINA AND THE LAGUNA ATASCOSA NWR. ROAD BED HAD BEEN PARTIALLY WASHED AWAY ON FM 106 WITH DEEP STANDING WATER EITHER SIDE OF THE ROADWAY. WATER REMAINED IN OR UP TO THE EDGE OF SOME HOMES. WATER REMAINED OVER HIGHWAY 1847. NUMEROUS VEHICLES HAD BEEN DISABLED BY FLOOD WATERS ON FM 1847.
OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS 6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ON ROAD IN IMMEDIATE VICINITY OF LA TINA. ALSO SEVERAL CARS STALLED OUT AND EVIDENCE OF WATER UP TO HOMES.
RINCON DEL SAN JOSE TCOON SITE GUSTED TO 38 KNOTS.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS A FEW OLDER HOUSES HAD ROOFS PARTIALLY REMOVED SEVERAL METAL BUILDINGS TRAILERS HAD ROOFS TOTALLY REMOVED...A CHAIN LINK FENCE WAS BLOWN DOWN...NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN THROUGH THE CITY AND A LARGE OBJECT BLEW OFF OF A CONSTRUCTION SITE AT THE COURTHOUSE AND DAMAGED NEARBY OBJECTS. HAIL TO AROUND PING PONG BALL SIZE WAS ALSO DOCUMENTED. WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 65-70 MPH.
ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOUSE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 5TH AND HIDALGO IN ZAPATA.
ZAPATA COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN THE TOWN OF ZAPATA.
WIND GUST OF 50 KNOTS REPORT BY THE TCOON SITE AT RINCON DEL SAN JOSE AT 836 PM.
BUOY020 REPORTED A WIND GUSTS TO 39 KNOTS AT 10 PM.
